candidates.notes: All setup_env pass criteria met. conda env sibyl_CRA created with Python 3.11. PyTorch 2.5.1+cu121 with CUDA on 4x RTX 4090.

failed_assumptions: H2_contrastive_gain (zero gain at pilot scale -- rank-based metrics invariant to mean shift)
H7_whitened_attribution (degrades all tasks by 8-11pp at N/d=0.049)
H4_r_eff_in_256_1024 (full-model grad r_eff=10, much lower than predicted)
candidates.notes: CRA thesis is PARTIALLY SUPPORTED with significant caveats requiring narrative revision. FM1 (signal dilution) has strong spectral evidence and is confirmed on attribution tasks (counterfact, ftrace). FM2 (common influence contamination) is UNTESTED at pilot scale because rank-based metrics are invariant to contrastive mean-subtraction. H7 whitened attribution fails due to underdetermined covariance (N/d=0.049). Toxicity task shows a gradient-norm artifact where parameter-space methods dominate. Several quantitative predictions (H4, H9) need revision but qualitative direction supports FM1.

H1_FM1_space_gap.notes: Strong on attribution tasks; reversed on toxicity (gradient norm artifact)

H2_contrastive_asymmetry.root_cause: Mean-subtraction preserves rank ordering exactly; rank-based metrics (AUPRC, R@K) invariant
H2_contrastive_asymmetry.recommendation: Full-scale needs continuous metrics (Kendall tau on raw scores) or larger sample to break ties

H3_orthogonality.notes: No FM2 main effect detected, so interaction untestable
H3_orthogonality.recommendation: Requires H2 to produce nonzero FM2 effect first

H4_gradient_r_eff.notes: Gradient signal far more concentrated than predicted; STRENGTHENS FM1 argument
H4_gradient_r_eff.recommendation: Reframe from 'r_eff ~ O(d)' to 'r_eff << d << B'

H5_trak_saturation.notes: Saturation exists but TRAK-PCA at k=d still 30.8pp below RepSim, suggesting factors beyond projection dimension

H6_kfac_control.notes: RepSim > K-FAC IF by 17.4pp on counterfact. On toxicity, Raw Dot IF (no Hessian) achieves 0.94 AUPRC

H7_whitened_attribution.root_cause: N/d ratio = 0.049; covariance estimation severely underdetermined
H7_whitened_attribution.positive_signals: SNR concept directionally validated (positive correlation); no numerical issues
H7_whitened_attribution.recommendation: Needs N >> d, PCA-reduced whitening, or oracle covariance

H8_repsim_vs_bm25.notes: BM25 achieves R@50=1.0 on counterfact at pilot scale (likely lexically solvable)

H9_condition_numbers.notes: Direction completely reversed. Rep covariance rank-deficient at N=100 < d=512

key_findings: FM1 main effect strong on counterfact (+32pp) and ftrace (+17pp), reversed on toxicity (-24pp)
FM2 main effect is exactly zero (contrastive scoring preserves rank ordering)
Interaction term trivially zero (no FM2 main effect to interact with)
BM25 dominates counterfact at pilot scale (R@50=1.0); kNN competitive on toxicity (0.809)

trak_dim_sweep.pca_vs_random: PCA underperforms random at k=256 (-9.9pp), matches at k=1024

repsim_dim_sweep.notes: RepSim saturated at just 64 PCA dims (N=100 < d=2048 prevents testing higher dims). Representations have at most ~100 significant components at pilot scale.

h6_gate.reason: H6 confirmed on counterfact (+17.4pp). Toxicity reversal is gradient norm artifact, not H6 failure.

h3_gate.reason: Interaction is trivially 0 because FM2 main effect is 0 (contrastive invariance at pilot scale)

bm25_gate.reason: BM25 R@50=1.0 on counterfact (likely lexically solvable at N=100). Need full-scale to determine if persistent.

critical_issues_for_full_scale.cause: Rank-based metrics invariant to additive constant; mean-subtraction is an additive constant
critical_issues_for_full_scale.fix: Add continuous metrics: Kendall-tau on raw scores, Spearman rho, or score-level NDCG

critical_issues_for_full_scale.issue: H7 whitened attribution degrades all tasks
critical_issues_for_full_scale.fix: Full scale N/d should be 2-5x; add PCA-reduced whitening (whiten in top-k eigenspace)

critical_issues_for_full_scale.cause: Gradient norm artifact (unsafe samples have systematically higher loss gradients)
critical_issues_for_full_scale.fix: Frame as task-type dependent phenomenon; not a CRA failure but a scope boundary

critical_issues_for_full_scale.issue: H4 quantitative prediction wrong (r_eff=10 vs predicted 256-1024)
critical_issues_for_full_scale.cause: Gradient signal even more concentrated than theory predicted
critical_issues_for_full_scale.fix: Reframe: strengthens FM1 argument; update theory to 'r_eff << d << B'

critical_issues_for_full_scale.cause: Pilot N=100 < d=512 creates rank-deficient covariance
critical_issues_for_full_scale.fix: Reframe using spectral concentration metrics instead of raw condition numbers

critical_issues_for_full_scale.issue: TRAK dim sweep non-monotonic and smoking gun fails (30.8pp gap)
critical_issues_for_full_scale.cause: Factors beyond projection dimension (curvature, layer selection, FM2) create persistent gap
critical_issues_for_full_scale.fix: Acknowledge in paper; position as evidence that FM1 is necessary but not sufficient

risks_identified.risk: Toxicity AUPRC tests gradient norm magnitude, not attribution quality
risks_identified.impact: CRA claims about FM1 may not apply to binary detection tasks
risks_identified.mitigation: Frame toxicity as scope boundary; focus evidence on counterfact/ftrace

risks_identified.risk: FM2 (contrastive scoring) has zero empirical evidence at pilot scale
risks_identified.impact: Half of the CRA thesis (FM2 independence, contrastive as universal fix) is unvalidated
risks_identified.mitigation: Full-scale with continuous metrics is ESSENTIAL; cannot publish without FM2 evidence

risks_identified.risk: H7 whitened attribution may not work even at full scale
risks_identified.impact: Weakens the prescriptive framework contribution (optimal M selection)
risks_identified.mitigation: PCA-reduced whitening; frame as open direction if still fails

risks_identified.risk: Small pilot N=100 distorts multiple analyses
risks_identified.mitigation: Full experiment uses complete training pool (5K-10K samples)

risks_identified.impact: H4, H9 specific numbers wrong; need reframing
risks_identified.mitigation: Update paper claims to match evidence; strengthen qualitative FM1 narrative

h6_decision_gate.revision_reason: Toxicity AUPRC is not a valid H6 test. On counterfact (genuine attribution), H6 passes strongly: RepSim > K-FAC IF by 17.4pp.
